author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Big Walk's creators reflect on their six-year journey

The mandatory COVID lockdowns of 2020 inspired Big Walk. Melbourne-based developer House House – which was behind the 2019 hit Untitled Goose Game – wanted to create something that was about socialising outside with your friends at a time when this was impossible. But the devs had no idea it would take six years to complete.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Apple announces Europe's first Developer Centre

Apple plans to open its first European Developer Centre later this year.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Date Everything wins two accolades at The Gayming Awards 2026

Sassy Chap Games' Date Everything won Best LGBTQ+ Indie Game and the Gayming Fan Favourite Award at this year's Gayming Awards.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
"Product visibility needs a more cohesive and structural approach" – How VaultN aims to unify the digital distribution ecosystem

Last year, the digital distribution platform VaultN partnered with Take-Two Interactive, having already secured customers such as Bethesda, Team17, Landfall, and Curve.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Amazon committed to developing Lord of the Rings game, is "evaluating different concepts"

Amazon remains committed to developing a Lord of the Rings game, despite recent reports of its MMO project being cancelled.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Türkiye's domestic mobile revenue grew 6% to $347m in 2025

Türkiye's mobile revenue rose 6% in 2025 to $347 million, its highest annual total to date. Over the past six years, Turkish developer revenue has grown by 450% to $2.7 billion.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Ninja Theory cancels Project Mara

Ninja Theory has cancelled its horror title codenamed Project Mara.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Roblox partners with SuperAwesome to bring "brand safe advertising" to under-13s

Youth marketing firm SuperAwesome has been appointed Roblox's commercial and technology partner for "under-13 contextual advertising."

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Xbox explains new exclusives policy: players "can expect a reliable pipeline" of exclusive titles in service of "turning around the business"

Xbox Chief Strategy Officer Matthew Ball has given more insight into the firm's new commitment to platform exclusives, following the announcement yesterday that both Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution would not be available on non-Xbox platforms.

Read more
